mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth on forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation is not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive forward 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and high federal funds rate suggests tight monetary conditions that squeeze corporate margins and reduce equity valuation multiples, producing negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL is above 200 and FEDFUNDS is above 4%, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the joint signal has no significant negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment combined with rising unemployment indicates a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: When the joint trigger fires, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of quarters, or the average return is not at least -2%.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which supports economic expansion and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1500, forward 20-day return median is not positive, or the positive hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth signals robust manufacturing activity and supply-side momentum, which lifts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.04 over any 5-year period, or the signal is not directionally consistent in expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, further rate hikes signal a tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following trigger, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 45% of occurrences, or the average excess return over the risk-free rate is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales above the three-month moving average indicates strong consumer demand, which boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon, but only when inflation remains moderate.
failure_condition: When the joint trigger condition is met, forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive at the 95% confidence level.
